questions.question: What is a variable?
options: A named storage location in memory
questions.answer: A named storage location in memory

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a primitive data type?
What is the purpose of data types?
options: To define the size of a variable
To define the type of value a variable can hold

questions.question: What data type is used to store whole numbers?
What data type is used to store decimal numbers?
Which data type represents a truth value (True or False)?
What data type is used to store text?
What does 'declaration' of a variable mean?
options: Assigning a value to a variable
Specifying the name and data type of a variable
Converting a variable to a different data type
questions.answer: Specifying the name and data type of a variable

questions.question: What does 'initialization' of a variable mean?
options: Specifying the name and data type of a variable
Assigning a value to a variable for the first time
Converting a variable to a different data type
questions.answer: Assigning a value to a variable for the first time

questions.question: What is the difference between a local and a global variable?
options: Local variables can only be used in one function, global variables can be used anywhere in the program.
Global variables can only be used in one function, local variables can be used anywhere in the program.
Local variables are always integers, global variables are always strings.
questions.answer: Local variables can only be used in one function, global variables can be used anywhere in the program.

questions.question: What is the scope of a variable?
options: The data type of the variable
The region of the program where the variable can be accessed
The initial value of the variable
questions.answer: The region of the program where the variable can be accessed

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a valid variable name (assuming typical programming rules)?
What is type conversion?
options: Converting a variable to a different data type
Changing the name of a variable
Assigning a value to a variable
questions.answer: Converting a variable to a different data type

questions.question: What is implicit type conversion?
options: Conversion performed automatically by the compiler/interpreter
Conversion that is always an error
Conversion that only works for integers
questions.answer: Conversion performed automatically by the compiler/interpreter

questions.question: What is explicit type conversion?
options: Conversion performed automatically by the compiler/interpreter
Conversion that is always an error
Conversion that only works for integers

questions.question: What can happen if you try to store a value that is too large for a variable's data type?
options: The value will be truncated (data loss).
The variable will automatically change data type.
questions.answer: The value will be truncated (data loss).

questions.question: What is a constant?
options: A variable that can be changed during program execution
A value that cannot be changed during program execution
A function that always returns the same value
questions.answer: A value that cannot be changed during program execution

questions.question: Which data type is typically used to store a single character?
What is an array?
options: A single variable that can hold one value
A collection of variables of the same data type, stored contiguously in memory
A function that returns multiple values
questions.answer: A collection of variables of the same data type, stored contiguously in memory

questions.question: What is a pointer?
options: A variable that stores the value of another variable
A variable that stores the memory address of another variable
A variable that can only store boolean values
questions.answer: A variable that stores the memory address of another variable

questions.question: Which of the following is a compound data type?
What is the purpose of assigning a data type to a variable?
options: To allocate the correct amount of memory.
To tell the compiler how to interpret the data stored in the variable.
To enforce type safety and prevent errors.

questions.question: What happens if you try to assign a string value to an integer variable?
options: The program will throw a type error.
The string will be automatically converted to an integer.
The integer variable will become a string.
questions.answer: The program will throw a type error.

questions.question: What is a strongly typed language?
options: A language where the data type of a variable must be explicitly declared.
A language that automatically converts data types.
A language where variables can change their data type during runtime.
A language that doesn't have any data types.
questions.answer: A language where the data type of a variable must be explicitly declared.

questions.question: What is a weakly typed language?
options: A language where the data type of a variable must be explicitly declared.
A language that automatically converts data types.
A language where variables cannot change their data type during runtime.
A language that doesn't have any data types.
questions.answer: A language that automatically converts data types.

questions.question: Which data type is used to store true or false values in Python?
In JavaScript, which keyword is used to declare a variable?
In Java, what is the difference between `int` and `Integer`?
options: `int` is a primitive type, `Integer` is a class.
`Integer` is a primitive type, `int` is a class.
questions.answer: `int` is a primitive type, `Integer` is a class.

questions.question: Which operator is used for assignment in most programming languages?
What does NaN stand for?
What data type would you use to store someone's age?
What data type would you use to store someone's name?
Which of the following can store multiple data types?
What is a null value?
options: A value indicating no value is present
A value that causes an error
questions.answer: A value indicating no value is present

questions.question: What is a volatile variable?
options: A variable that changes its value randomly
A variable that can be accessed by multiple threads simultaneously
A variable that is stored on disk
A variable that is not used in the program
questions.answer: A variable that can be accessed by multiple threads simultaneously

questions.question: In C++, what keyword is used to declare a constant variable?
What is the range of values for a boolean data type?
Which of the following data types is NOT supported in Python?
What is the size of an integer data type in a 32-bit system?
What is the size of a float data type in most systems?
What is the difference between `float` and `double`?
options: They are both used for strings.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the `static` keyword in Java?
options: To indicate that a variable or method belongs to the class rather than an instance of the class
questions.answer: To indicate that a variable or method belongs to the class rather than an instance of the class

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a literal?
What is the difference between a character and a string?
options: A character is a single letter, a string is a sequence of characters.
A string is a single letter, a character is a sequence of characters.
Characters are integers and strings are floats.
questions.answer: A character is a single letter, a string is a sequence of characters.

questions.question: What is the use of `enum` data type?
options: To define a set of named integer constants.
To define a set of string constants.
To create a complex data structure
questions.answer: To define a set of named integer constants.

questions.question: Which of the following is used to define an integer in C#?
Which of the following is a valid String declaration in Java?
Which symbol is commonly used to concatenate strings?
What is the purpose of casting?
options: To change the case of a string
To convert one data type to another
questions.answer: To convert one data type to another
